##NAMES=Picaite
##RRUFFID=R250053
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaCa[AsO_3_OH][AsO_2_(OH)_2_]
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless thick blades crystals typically in parallel intergrowths in association with  camanchacaite (R250031), chinchorroite (R250032),  espadaite (R250051),  magnesiofluckite (R250052) and ríosecoite (R250054). Type sample described in Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250053
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.00_Ca_0.98_Mg_0.02_)[AsO_3_OH][AsO_2_(OH)_2_]
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.2474 b: 14.6547 c: 7.2624 alpha: 90 beta: 99.52 gamma: 90 volume: 760.7 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
